system.description:
Templars are the greatly feared disciples of the sorcerer-kings. The work they perform is governed by endless bureaucracy, treachery and religion. They operate as law enforcers in their cities and are devoted to a single sorcerer-king.

Holy Symbol: A templar must carry a royal holy symbol

Spell Casting: Once a templar has advanced in the hierarchy (from 2nd level), the character may pray to receive spells from the Sphere of the Cosmos

Magic Item Creation: A templar of any level may spend time and money on magical research. When a Templar reaches 9th level, they are able to create magic items

Judgment: A templar can pass judgment on his city-state, arresting, investigating, sentencing, or pardoning as he sees fit. At 1st level: judgment on slaves, 6th level: judgment on free men, 12th level: judgment on nobles

Soldier Aid: A templar may request the help of 1d4 soldiers from his city-state at any time, of any level no higher than the templar

Nobility: A templar may be granted a land and title such as vizier. The land under the templar's control becomes part of his noble house

class.weapons: Clubs, daggers, shortbow, short swords and staves
